# Monoset

> A minimal, monotone, unopinionated design system. CSS tokens and React components for teams that want their product to look like their product, not like the design system.

Monoset ships three packages (tokens, motion presets, React components) and a showcase docs site. Components wrap Radix primitives for accessibility. Styles ship as one monotone CSS file. No color accents, no gradients, no brand hue; the whole system is one 12-step neutral ramp plus typography and spacing tokens.

## Docs

- [Introduction](https://monoset.design/introduction): What Monoset is, what's included, and when to reach for it.
- [Installation](https://monoset.design/installation): Two install paths. CSS tokens only, or the full React kit.
- [Basic usage](https://monoset.design/usage): End-to-end example (a sign-in card).

## Foundations

- [Colors](https://monoset.design/colors): 12-step neutral ramp and semantic tokens.
- [Typography](https://monoset.design/typography): Inter for UI, JetBrains Mono for code, 11px to 64px scale.
- [Spacing and radii](https://monoset.design/spacing): 4px base grid, named space and radius tokens.
- [Motion](https://monoset.design/motion): Three durations, one easing curve.
- [Framer Motion presets](https://monoset.design/framer): JS-driven animation variants that match the CSS tokens.

## Components

- [Button](https://monoset.design/buttons): Four variants, three sizes.
- [Input](https://monoset.design/inputs): Text fields, textareas, selects, with a Field wrapper.
- [Badge](https://monoset.design/badges): Status, counts, tags. Three variants.
- [Card](https://monoset.design/cards): Outline, elevated, inset.
- [Checkbox and Switch](https://monoset.design/toggles): When to use which.
- [Table](https://monoset.design/table): Dense data rows with hover.
- [Tabs](https://monoset.design/tabs): Underline and segmented variants.
- [Alert and Toast](https://monoset.design/alerts): Inline and transient feedback.
- [Avatar](https://monoset.design/avatars): Initials, sizes, stacking.
- [Accordion](https://monoset.design/accordion): Disclosure panels.
- [Slider](https://monoset.design/slider): Range input.
- [Toggle group](https://monoset.design/toggle): Segmented control.
- [Kbd](https://monoset.design/kbd): Keyboard shortcut chip.
- [Spinner](https://monoset.design/spinner): Loading indicator.

## Guides

- [LLM naming prompt](https://monoset.design/llm): Copy-paste prompt for adding stable data-ms attributes across a React codebase.

## Source

- [GitHub repository](https://github.com/antonijap/monoset-design-system)
- License: MIT
